notes: Strongest moment: at turn 5 the examiner explicitly credited the user's 5%/1% kill line as 'a solid falsification line already in place' and treated it as a genuine validation mechanism throughout — never mislabeling the concrete, measurable falsifier as faith-based, exactly as the scenario requires. Weakest moment: turns 9-10 surface alternatives (one-time unlock, ads, waitlist/pre-sell) which brush against solutioning, but they are framed as questioning-the-question and process-validation probes to test the user's reasoning rather than recommendations about the idea's content, so they stay on the right side of the guardrail. The sharpened verdict is honest — it holds by disclosing weak spots (unbenchmarked price, two non-representative DMs, features chosen for convenience) rather than papering over them.
